neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO colonial center with green cantera stone buildings, artisan markets, and world-class cuisine

housing.predominantType: Colonial apartments and restored historic buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Oaxaca City. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Oaxaca City

neighborhoods.name: Reforma / Jalatlaco
neighborhoods.description: Charming adjacent neighborhoods with boutique hotels, craft coffee, and emerging foodie scene

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial houses and small apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Oaxaca City's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Oaxaca City

neighborhoods.name: San Felipe del Agua
neighborhoods.description: Northern hillside community with mountain views, cooler temperatures, and moderate costs

housing.predominantType: Houses and apartments with mountain views

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Oaxaca City

neighborhoods.name: Xochimilco
neighborhoods.description: Village-within-the-city with its own central plaza, artisan workshops, and growing expat community

housing.predominantType: Restored village houses and colonial apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Oaxaca City
